_comment: Source whitelist + URL templates. confidence (critical/high/medium) feeds Phase 1 source-confidence labeling. {ticker}/{cik} are template variables.

edgar.notes: 8-K filings = official material events. Fastest legal source; highest confidence.

finviz.notes: Aggregator; high editorial filter. Requires User-Agent header.

competitor_finviz.feeds_into: data collection only — pipeline forces verdict=REVIEW
competitor_finviz.notes: T1 data collection (commit 2026-04-28). Iterates each ticker's competitor_tickers list (tickers.json) and pulls Finviz news for each competitor. Items get ticker_hint=target, source=competitor_finviz; pipeline routes to REVIEW without LLM. Run for ≥1 week, then analyze qc/daily-report and processed-log to decide whether to crystallize patterns into competitor-impact-rules.json (T2).

google_news.notes: Topic/keyword-driven. Used for non-ticker-specific signal (FDA news, DOE policy, uranium market). Lowest precision; relies hardest on LLM oracle. Items from aggregator publishers (MSN, Yahoo Finance) are routed to REVIEW by pipeline._is_aggregator — re-syndication timestamps are unreliable and SPA pages defeat body extraction.

pr_newswire.notes: First-party press-release wires (GlobeNewswire / PR Newswire / Business Wire) reached via Google News with site: filter. Full-text-friendly: every URL points to a primary source the body fetcher can reliably extract. Per-ticker queries derived from tickers.json company_aliases automatically.

finnhub_news.notes: Ticker-bound JSON API; returns {headline, summary, url, datetime, source}. The summary field (~200-500 chars) is enough LLM context to skip the article-body scrape battle. Free tier: 60 calls/min, US tickers only. Skipped if FINNHUB_API_KEY not set.
